title: Figma adds code layers, support for animations, more AI features in new update
slides: Figma’s latest update—just another iteration or a workflow revolution?
Now your designs can generate production-ready code layers.
But it also introduces native animation tools directly in the canvas.
You can skip handoff entirely and prototype with live logic.
Try rebuilding one component with code layers today.
thread_text:
1. Workflow or trap?

Figma’s new update generates production-ready code layers directly from designs.

2. Animation inside the canvas

Native motion tools let you prototype without leaving the editor for the first time.

3. Live logic in prototypes

Skip handoff entirely by wiring real UI behavior before developers touch it.

4. One component, two realities

A rebuilt button with code layers stays synced — design and code no longer diverge.

5. Test this week

Rebuild one component with code layers. Does it save time or just add another tool to audit?
video_script: Figma now turns designs into live code layers.
You can animate directly on the canvas.
No more handoff — prototype with real logic.
Design and code finally stay in sync.

script: Figma now turns designs into live code layers.
You can animate directly on the canvas.
No more handoff — prototype with real logic.
Design and code finally stay in sync.

instagram.caption:
Production-ready code layers now come directly from your Figma designs — no developer handoff required. 🛠️

The update adds native animation tools inside the canvas, so you never leave the editor to prototype motion. It also wires live logic into prototypes, letting you test real UI behavior before a single line of code is written.

You skip the handoff step entirely and keep one component synced between design and code. ⏯️

Rebuild one component with code layers this week — does it save you time or just add another tool to audit? 🔍

carousel_slides.description: Figma now generates production-ready code layers from designs, potentially saving hours of handoff work.
Native motion tools let you prototype directly in the canvas, eliminating the need for external animation software.
Skip handoff entirely by wiring real UI behavior inside Figma before developers touch a single line.
A rebuilt button with code layers stays in sync, so design and code never diverge again.
